# test/database — Part 1 of 2: core DB + conversions + journal_db + state

**Scope:** `test/database/{database_test.dart, maintenance_test.dart,
slow_query_logging_test.dart, editor_db_test.dart, conversions_test.dart,
common_test.dart, fts5_db_test.dart, settings_db_test.dart,
notifications_db_test.dart, open_db_connection_test.dart,
journal_db_actor_isolate_test.dart, column_exists_test.dart,
journal_update_result_test.dart, journal_db/config_flags_test.dart,
state/config_flag_provider_test.dart}` plus their implementation counterparts
under `lib/database/`.

**Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis)**

> **Resolution status (2026-06-05):** the split items and most quality/coverage
> items below are done — see the `[x]` checkboxes. `lib/database/database.dart`
> is now a shell (constructor + migration ladder) with ten `part` files holding
> private mixins; `database_test.dart` was mirror-split into per-mixin test
> files with shared infrastructure in `test_utils.dart`. Deliberately not done:
> splitting `slow_query_logging_test.dart` / `maintenance_test.dart` (would
> violate the one-test-file-per-source rule), and the per-group `setUpAll`
> speed consolidations (risk of cross-test state leaks; the per-file split
> already lets the suite shard). The `journalEntityMapForIds` chunk invariant
> is covered by an example-based 501-id boundary test rather than a Glados
> property.

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/database.dart` (3 602 lines) is the largest non-generated source file. It is a single `JournalDb` class with no section extensions. Natural split seams follow the method groupings already visible in the file:

  - **`journal_db_queries.dart`** (lines ~1 142–2 600) — all journal entity read methods (`getJournalEntities`, `getJournalEntitiesForIds`, `getJournalEntityMapForIds`, `getCategoryIdsForEntryIds`, etc.). Extractable as a private mixin or companion class `JournalDbQueries`.
  - **`task_queries.dart`** (lines ~1 468–1 907, ~2 442–2 600) — `getTasks`, `getTasksSortedByDueDate`, `_buildTasksByDueDateQuery`, `getFilteredTasksCount`, `getFilteredTaskIds`, `getInProgressTasks`, `getMissedRecurringTasks`, task-due coalescing (~2 813–2 985). This cluster already grows to ~700 lines.
  - **`project_queries.dart`** (lines ~2 641–2 985) — already marked with a section comment `// ── Project queries ──────────────────`.
  - **`links_ratings.dart`** (lines ~3 018–3 315) — `linksForEntryIds`, `basicLinksForEntryIds`, coalescing, rating queries.
  - **`definitions_config.dart`** (lines ~2 319–2 432, ~3 138–3 470) — config flags, entity-definition upserts, label definitions.

  None of these splits require a public API change; the methods are all on `JournalDb` and can be pulled into `extension` on `JournalDb` in separate files using Dart's `part`/`extension` pattern or companion mixins.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/database/database_test.dart` (8 642 lines) is the single largest test file in the repo by a large margin. It contains one mega-group `'Database Tests - '` (lines 334–8 084, ~7 750 lines, ~219 tests) plus one migration group (lines 8 091–8 642). Mirror-split to match the proposed impl split:

  - **`database_journal_queries_test.dart`** → `group('Journal queries -')` (line 548) through `group('getCategoryIdsForEntryIds -')` (line 2 775) + `group('Aggregate queries -')` (line 3 151) — ~1 000 lines.
  - **`database_task_queries_test.dart`** → `group('Task queries -')` (line 739) through ~line 2 224 + `group('getTasks with label/sortByDate/priority branch coverage -')` (line 5 035) + `group('getTasksSortedByDueDate edge cases -')` (line 5 414) + task-due coalescing section — ~2 200 lines.
  - **`database_journal_entity_ops_test.dart`** → `group('Journal Entity Operations -')` (line 5 647) + `group('Conflict Handling -')` (line 5 739) + `group('JSON persistence -')` (line 372) + `group('Edge cases -')` (line 440) + vector clock streaming (line 4 121) — ~800 lines.
  - **`database_links_ratings_test.dart`** → `group('Entry links -')` (line 3 614) + `group('Rating queries -')` (line 3 788) + `group('entryLinkById -')` (line 4 802) + `group('getBulkLinkedTimeSpans -')` (line 6 212) — ~800 lines.
  - **`database_projects_test.dart`** → `group('Project queries -')` (line 6 453) + related private-flag branches (line 7 607) — ~900 lines.
  - **`database_config_upsert_test.dart`** → `group('Upsert helpers -')` (line 2 989) + `group('Watch streams -')` (line 2 913) + config flags bootstrap (line 7 986) + config flags group — ~500 lines.
  - **`database_definitions_test.dart`** → label/category/habit/dashboard definition queries + label reconciliation (line 5 993) + label usage counts — ~600 lines.
  - **`database_migration_v18_test.dart`** → existing `group('JournalDb early migration (v18 -> current) -')` (line 8 091, ~550 lines) — already self-contained.

  The shared top-level helpers (`buildJournalEntry`, `createJournalEntryWithVclock`, `_testDate`, etc.) defined below line ~8 150 should move to a `test/database/test_utils.dart` factory file.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/slow_query_logging_test.dart` (1 323 lines) is over the 1 000-line guideline. Split seam: separate the `SlowQueryLogEntry` + `SlowQueryLoggingGate` unit group (lines ~18–103) from the `SlowQueryInterceptor` integration group (lines ~105–1 321) into `slow_query_log_entry_test.dart` and `slow_query_interceptor_test.dart`.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed — keep as one file) `lib/database/slow_query_logging.dart` is a single source file (`SlowQueryLogEntry`, `SlowQueryInterceptor`, `SlowQueryLoggingGate`, `_SlowQueryFileSink` all live in it). The "one test file per source file" rule takes precedence over the soft 1000-line guideline — splitting would create two test files mapping to one source. The size is driven by thorough interceptor integration coverage, not duplication.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/maintenance_test.dart` (1 076 lines) is just over threshold. Split seam: the `reSyncInterval – agent entities and links` sub-group (line 425–764, ~340 lines) could become `maintenance_agent_resync_test.dart`. The `purgeSentOutboxItems` group (line 976–end, ~100 lines) can stay in the main file.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed — keep as one file) `lib/database/maintenance.dart` is a single source file (the `Maintenance` class); splitting its test would violate one-test-file-per-source for a 76-line overage. Kept whole.

- [x] **[LOW]** `test/database/editor_db_test.dart` (726 lines) is below the 1 000-line guideline and well-structured.
  - **RESOLVED:** (assessed, no change) — confirmed 726 lines, under the guideline; no split needed.

## Test quality improvements

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/database/database_test.dart:8075` — "database operations are covered by tests" is a pure smoke test: it asserts `isNotNull` on `db` and `listConfigFlags()`. This adds zero value; `isNotNull` on a variable just instantiated in `setUp` is a constructor smoke test, which the guidelines explicitly forbid. Remove and distribute real assertions to the groups that exercise those paths.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/database/state/config_flag_provider_test.dart` — tests "returns false when flag not found" (line ~57) and "returns false when flag status is false" (line ~82) are near-identical: same mock stub, same container setup, same assertion. Only the test name differs. Merge into a single parameterized case or collapse one into the other.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `test/database/common_test.dart:203` — `await Future<void>.delayed(const Duration(milliseconds: 10))` is a real-time wait, violating the Fake Time Policy. The test "multiple backups create separate files" only needs two distinct file names, not a timestamp difference. The simplest fix is to call `createDbBackup` with two *different* source filenames, or to use a counter-based filename rather than relying on wall-clock milliseconds.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/common_test.dart` — `group('openDbConnection Tests')` (lines 235–307) contains several tests that assert only `isNotNull` on the returned `LazyDatabase` instance or assert the `toString()` contains `'LazyDatabase'`. These are constructor smoke tests with no behavioral value (`'lazy initialization behavior'`, `'different file names create separate connections'`, `'multiple calls with same filename return new instances'`). Replace with the behavioral tests already present in `open_db_connection_test.dart` (WAL mode, pragma values, directory creation) which are the correct assertions.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/slow_query_logging_test.dart:10` — `MockQueryExecutor` is defined inline (`class MockQueryExecutor extends Mock implements QueryExecutor {}`). Since `QueryExecutor` is a Drift public type and the mock is used in 5+ places across this file, it should be added to `test/mocks/mocks.dart` per the "Use centralized mocks" rule.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/fts5_db_test.dart:19` and `test/database/maintenance_test.dart:40` — both define `class _MockPathProviderPlatform extends Mock with MockPlatformInterfaceMixin implements PathProviderPlatform {}` identically. Add a single `MockPathProviderPlatform` (or `_MockPathProviderPlatform`) to a shared helper in `test/helpers/` or `test/mocks/mocks.dart` and import it from both files.

- [x] **[MED]** `test/database/database_test.dart` — the `setUp` on line 335 registers 6+ GetIt singletons, stubs 3+ mock methods, and opens a fresh in-memory DB for every one of the ~219 tests in the mega-group. The `mockUpdateNotifications`, `mockLoggingService`, and `testDirectory` setup is identical in every case. Extract this into a file-level helper `_DbTestBench` that is created once in `setUpAll` and reset (not re-created) in `setUp`. See "Use test bench classes for complex state" guideline.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/database/journal_db/config_flags_test.dart` — opens a fresh `JournalDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` for each of the 5 tests in the only group. The DB state is never shared between tests (each test calls `initConfigFlags` at the start). A single `setUpAll`-created instance that is re-initialized via `initConfigFlags` would cut 4 unnecessary DB open/close cycles while keeping full isolation.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/journal_db_actor_isolate_test.dart` — the single test uses 40+ lines of mock stubs for `mockLoggingService` (3 `when()` calls for `.log` and `.error`) that are identical to the pattern in `database_test.dart`. Extract to a shared `_stubDomainLogger(mock)` helper.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/column_exists_test.dart` — opens a file-based `JournalDb(overriddenFilename: 'col_exists.db')` (line 49) rather than an in-memory instance. This is intentional (testing real schema migration), but the test also goes through a full platform channel mock for path_provider (lines 23–33), repeating the same boilerplate seen in `journal_db/config_flags_test.dart`. Extract the path channel mock to a shared test utility.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/conversions.dart` — `fromDbEntity` has a priority-patch branch (lines 120–131): when `dbEntity.taskPriority` is non-null and non-empty, it calls `taskPriorityFromString` and merges the result back. The round-trip `toDbEntity → fromDbEntity` should be a Glados property: for any `JournalEntity`, `fromDbEntity(toDbEntity(entity)).meta.id == entity.meta.id` and for Task variants, the priority round-trips correctly. No such property exists in `conversions_test.dart` — all tests are example-based. A generator over `JournalEntity` variants (re-using the existing `_taskEntry`/`_measurementEntry` builder shapes) would cover the `maybeMap` completeness, the geolocation copy path, and the taskPriority patch path across all priorities. Tag `glados`.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/database.dart` — `_extractVectorClock` (line 1353) and `_extractEntryLinkVectorClock` (line 1369) are pure static functions that parse JSON strings with multiple error paths (null meta, null vc, non-numeric values, `FormatException`). These are excellent Glados candidates: a generator that emits valid, partially-valid, and malformed JSON strings (mixing null fields, wrong types, invalid JSON) and asserts that the function always returns `null` for invalid input and the correct map for valid input. Only example-based tests exist (`'_extractEntryLinkVectorClock FormatException path -'` at line 4934). Tag `glados`.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/conversions.dart` — `_sanitizeDashboardJson` (lines 282–317) is a pure function: given a JSON map, it filters `items` by `_knownDashboardItemTypes`. A Glados property over arbitrary lists of runtimeType strings (mix of known and unknown) would assert: all known types pass through, all unknown types are removed, the returned list length equals the count of known-type items. Currently only 5 example-based tests exist covering specific item type names. Tag `glados`.
  - **RESOLVED:** added a `glados`-tagged property (numRuns 120) in `conversions_test.dart` exercising the public `fromDashboardDbEntity` path: for any interleaving of valid known items (measurement/healthChart/habitChart) and unknown runtimeTypes, the deserialized dashboard contains exactly the known-type count — unknowns are always dropped pre-deserialization.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/database.dart:125` — `_sqliteInListChunk = 500` chunking logic in `journalEntityMapForIds` (line 1 129) and `linksForEntryIds` (line 2 617) is a pure batch-splitting algorithm. A Glados property that generates id lists of varying lengths (0, 1, 499, 500, 501, 1001, 2347) and asserts that all ids are visited exactly once and the return map has exactly the number of matching entities would provide stronger guarantees than the existing 500-boundary examples in `batch_chunking_test.dart`. Tag `glados`. *(Note: `batch_chunking_test.dart` is Part 1 scope and already covers the 500-boundary with a concrete example; the Glados property would add stronger coverage for arbitrary sizes.)*
  - **RESOLVED:** added a `glados`-tagged property (numRuns 60) in `batch_chunking_test.dart` over `getDayPlansByIds` (which routes through the shared `_sqliteInListChunk = 500` splitter): with six stored rows, an arbitrary-length request id list (0..1100 ids, duplicates and absent ids, spanning multiple chunks) always returns exactly the stored∩requested set with no row repeated — proving every id is visited once regardless of chunk count.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/database/journal_update_result.dart` — `JournalUpdateSkipReason.label` is a pure exhaustive switch over an enum. A Glados property asserting that every `JournalUpdateSkipReason` value returns a non-empty string label would be a trivial but safe property. Currently only 4 example-based assertions exist. Tag `glados`.
  - **RESOLVED:** already done — `journal_update_result_test.dart:66-79` has a `glados`-tagged property (numRuns 120) over `any.skipReason` asserting `label` is non-empty for every enum value, alongside the 4 known-label examples.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/database.dart` — `getWorkouts()` (line 3 118) and `getAllMeasurableDataTypes()` (line 2 550) / `getMeasurableDataTypeById()` (line 2 545) have zero direct tests in `database_test.dart`. They are mocked in feature tests only. The `database_test.dart` `'Upsert helpers -'` group inserts a measurable type but never reads it back via `getAllMeasurableDataTypes`. Add at least two tests: one asserting `getAllMeasurableDataTypes()` returns inserted rows ordered by `displayName` (covered by `measurableDataTypeStreamMapper` sorting), and one for `getWorkouts` basic filter.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/conversions.dart` — `fromDbEntity` is not tested in `conversions_test.dart` at all. Every test calls `toDbEntity` one-way. The inverse path (deserialize from DB column, apply priority patch, return entity) is only indirectly covered by integration paths in `database_test.dart`. Add at least three direct unit tests in `conversions_test.dart`: (1) a Task entity where `taskPriority` column overrides the serialized priority, (2) a Task entity where priorities already match (no-op patch), (3) a non-Task entity (patch path never executes). These are pure-logic tests with no real DB.

- [x] **[HIGH]** `lib/database/database.dart` — the `_coalesceOpenTasksDueUpTo` path (lines 2 842–2 902, microtask coalescing for task-due queries) has no dedicated unit test that exercises the "multiple concurrent callers share one DB round-trip" behavior. The coalescing logic in `_coalesceEntityById` and `_coalesceCalendarEntries` have such tests; the task-due coalescer does not. This is a performance-critical path (DailyOS prefetch). Add a test that issues two `getTasksDueOn` calls in the same microtask and asserts `runTasksDueFetch` is invoked exactly once.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/conversions.dart` — `labelDefinitionDbEntity` and `fromLabelDefinitionDbEntity` are not tested in `conversions_test.dart` (only `categoryDefinitionDbEntity` / `linkedDbEntity` / `dashboardStreamMapper` are). Add round-trip coverage for label definitions including the `deleted → name = id` branch.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/database.dart` — `getHabitCompletionsInRange()` (line 2 589) has no test in `database_test.dart`. The closely related `getHabitCompletionsByHabitId()` does. Add a test asserting date-range boundary behavior (inclusive/exclusive endpoints).

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/database.dart` — `journalEntityMapForIds` over-500-id chunking (line 1 129) is not tested with more than a small set. `batch_chunking_test.dart` covers `getDayPlansByIds` and `runRatingsForTimeEntriesQueryForIds` across the 500-boundary, but not `journalEntityMapForIds`. Add a test that inserts 501 entries and asserts the returned map contains all 501.

- [x] **[MED]** `lib/database/database.dart` — constraint violation on `insertLabel` (duplicate `(journal_id, label_id)`) is tested (line 8 019), but the corresponding `SqliteException` handler in `insertLabel` (line 923) is the only place DB-level constraint errors are tested in the whole file. The `upsertEntryLink` precheck-error path is covered (line 4 826). The `addConflict` duplicate-id path is not explicitly tested (the `insertOrIgnore` semantics). Add a test for inserting the same conflict id twice and verifying no row duplication.

- [x] **[LOW]** `lib/database/notifications_db.dart` — `notificationsForLinkedEntity` returns ordered results (`scheduledFor DESC`). The existing test at line ~53 asserts the order `['upcoming', 'due']` (correct), but there is no test for an empty `linkedTaskId` result set or a task with 0 notifications. Add a "returns empty list for unknown linkedTaskId" test.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/column_exists_test.dart` — uses a real file-based DB (line 49) to test `columnExistsForTesting`. An in-memory DB with the current schema would work equally well and be faster. The file-based path is only necessary if testing migration scenarios, which this test does not.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/database/database_test.dart` — the `setUp` at line 335 opens a fresh `JournalDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` and calls `initConfigFlags` (which performs ~25 `INSERT OR IGNORE` DB writes) before **every** single one of ~219 tests. With even 5 ms per DB open + config flag seed, that is >1 second of pure setup overhead per run just for this group. Since the vast majority of tests are stateless with respect to config flags (they read the default `private = true`), the DB and flags could be initialized once in `setUpAll` and individual tests that mutate config flags should use `addTearDown` to reset the specific flag rather than relying on a full DB teardown. Estimated saving: >50% of this group's wall-clock time.

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `test/database/maintenance_test.dart:151` — `setUp` opens a fresh `JournalDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)`, registers 7 GetIt singletons, creates a temp directory, and initializes 3 additional service mocks for every test in the outer group (~35+ tests). The `journalDb` instance is never mutated in a way that would contaminate adjacent tests (inserts are to a fresh in-memory DB each time). Promote to `setUpAll` with per-test insert cleanup via `db.delete(db.journal).go()` for tests that insert rows.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/database/journal_db/config_flags_test.dart` — opens a new `JournalDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` in `setUp` for each of the 5 tests (lines 23–36). Since `initConfigFlags` is idempotent and none of the tests share state, a single `setUpAll`-opened DB with `initConfigFlags` called once and individual test-level toggles reverted via `addTearDown` would reduce 4 unnecessary open/close cycles.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/database/fts5_db_test.dart` — the `'insertText'` sub-group (lines 102–295) opens and closes a fresh `Fts5Db(inMemoryDatabase: true)` in its own `setUp`/`tearDown`, and the `'watchFullTextMatches'` sub-group (lines 296–405) does the same. Both are nested under the outer `'Fts5Db Tests'` group. Since `Fts5Db` schema has only one table (`journalFts`) and each test inserts unique entry IDs, a single DB instance with per-test `db.delete(db.journalFts).go()` cleanup in `tearDown` would halve the DB open/close count.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/database/settings_db_test.dart` — the single `setUp` creates a new `SettingsDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` for each of ~18 tests (lines 46–49), then `tearDown` closes it. The settings DB has no migration that would differ across tests; a shared instance reset by `customStatement("DELETE FROM settings")` per test would be both faster and valid since all tests start from an empty table.

- [ ] **[MED]** `test/database/notifications_db_test.dart` — same pattern: new `NotificationsDb(inMemoryDatabase: true, background: false)` per test in `setUp` (lines 9–11). 5 tests × DB open/close is small but follows the same pattern. Shared instance with per-test table truncation.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/column_exists_test.dart` — opens a **file-based** `JournalDb(overriddenFilename: 'col_exists.db')` which triggers the full schema migration on first open. An in-memory instance would be faster and would still exercise `_columnExists` against the current schema. The test does not test migration behavior; it tests the helper method on the live schema.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/editor_db_test.dart` — opens a new `EditorDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` in `setUp` (line 13) for each of ~35 tests. The `allDrafts()` query in the `'allDrafts Query Tests'` group (line 584+) never leaves persistent state. Promote to a shared instance with per-test cleanup where inserts are done.

- [ ] **[LOW]** `test/database/batch_chunking_test.dart` — opens a new `JournalDb(inMemoryDatabase: true)` and calls `initConfigFlags` in `setUp` for each test. The `getDayPlansByIds` group (5 tests) and `runRatingsForTimeEntriesQueryForIds` group (2 tests) could share a single DB instance initialized in `setUpAll` since the chunk-boundary tests are purely about query behavior (no config flag mutations).

---

## Summary

- **2 oversized implementation files**: `lib/database/database.dart` (3 602 lines, ~7× guideline) and `lib/database/slow_query_logging.dart` (427 lines — at guideline boundary).
- **2 oversized test files**: `test/database/database_test.dart` (8 642 lines, ~17× guideline) and `test/database/slow_query_logging_test.dart` (1 323 lines, ~2.6×), plus `maintenance_test.dart` (1 076, just over).
- **4 weak tests identified**: smoke-only "database operations covered" test in `database_test.dart:8075`; two near-identical `configFlagProvider` false-value tests; `Future.delayed` real-time wait in `common_test.dart:203`; multiple `openDbConnection` `isNotNull` smoke tests in `common_test.dart`.
- **4 Glados candidates**: `fromDbEntity` round-trip + `toDbEntity` completeness in `conversions.dart`; `_extractVectorClock` / `_extractEntryLinkVectorClock` malformed-JSON properties; `_sanitizeDashboardJson` item-filter property; `journalEntityMapForIds` chunk-size invariant.
- **8 speed wins identified**: the biggest is the `database_test.dart` setUp re-opening a fresh DB + seeding 25 config flags before each of ~219 tests — promoting to `setUpAll` could cut >50% of this group's wall-clock time; secondary wins from shared instances in `maintenance_test.dart`, `settings_db_test.dart`, `notifications_db_test.dart`, and `fts5_db_test.dart`.
- **Biggest single opportunity**: split `test/database/database_test.dart` (8 642 lines) into 7–8 mirror files matching the proposed `lib/database/database.dart` split — this would address both the file-size and the per-test DB-open speed issues simultaneously, since each sub-file can have a correctly scoped shared DB instance.
